runtime_context.compatibility_note: The supplied UDA-city dataset requested supy >= 2026.6.5, but this macOS 14 host could not install the available macOS 15 ARM wheel. The analysis used a clearly labelled compatibility path.

untracked_items.reason: Nested upstream dataset checkout intentionally left untracked.

session_summary.objective: Develop a SUEWS hackathon practice submission with baseline heat-risk analysis, physical-parameter experiments, a decision-maker dashboard, and transcripts.

turns.user_request: Write a comprehensive report for each site including site descriptions, socio-economic factors, physical factors, climatological factors, SUEWS results, plots, heat-risk calculation and heat risk.
assistant_actions: Ran present and future UDA-city SUEWS scenarios through the local compatibility path.
Used the supplied heat-risk bridge to calculate hazard, exposure, vulnerability and risk.
Generated compact CSV outputs and plots.
Created docs/report.md and linked it from docs/index.md.

key_results.risk_reason: High heat hazard, high exposure and high vulnerability coincide.

turns.user_request: Keep things simple and modify one physical SUEWS parameter at a time.
assistant_actions: Created a physical-parameter experiment runner.
Ran future-forcing experiments for tree fraction and albedo changes.
Saved compact experiment summaries and generated experiment config files.
Documented the findings in the report and experiment notes.

experiments.change: +0.05 evergreen tree surface fraction, -0.05 paved surface fraction
+0.10 evergreen tree surface fraction, -0.10 paved surface fraction

interpretation: Paved albedo was the strongest beneficial single-factor test.
Building albedo was beneficial but weaker.
Tree surface-fraction-only changes increased the dry-bulb air-temperature threshold metric, which was treated as a diagnostic result rather than evidence against urban trees.

turns.user_request: Discuss why increasing tree coverage resulted in more heat risk hours, despite an expectation that trees should increase shading and reduce absorbed radiation.
assistant_response_summary: The dangerous-heat metric used 2 m air temperature, not shaded pedestrian heat exposure or mean radiant temperature.
The model edit changed land-cover fractions, not explicit street shading geometry.
Vegetation can change albedo, heat storage, turbulence and flux partitioning.
The tree tests did not add irrigation, soil-water support, canopy geometry or revised morphology.
The result should be described as a narrow surface-fraction-only test, not a conclusion that trees increase heat risk.
turns.recommended_framing: The experiment does not show that trees increase heat risk; it shows that a surface-fraction-only tree retrofit is not a well-posed cooling intervention under this model setup and this air-temperature-only risk metric.

turns.user_request: Include baseline values as well as new experimental values in the report.
assistant_actions: Added baseline future dangerous-heat hours beside experiment values.
Added baseline risk and paved-albedo risk comparisons.
Added the albedo baseline note: paved and building albedo values were 0.20 in all sites and were tested at 0.35.

commit.message: Add baseline comparisons to experiment report

turns.user_request: Focus on variables other than albedo.
assistant_actions: Stopped an interrupted albedo-ladder run.
Added non-albedo physical experiments for grass, water, evergreen irrigation and vegetation soil-water storage.
Ran the new experiments under the future hot-humid forcing.
Updated the report and experiment notes.

experiments.change: +0.05 grass surface fraction, -0.05 paved surface fraction
+0.02 water surface fraction, -0.02 paved surface fraction
Evergreen tree irrigation fraction set to 1.0
Evergreen, deciduous and grass soil-store capacity set from 150 to 300

interpretation: Grass was nearly neutral but slightly worse.
Water was nearly neutral but slightly beneficial.
Evergreen irrigation alone did not change dangerous-heat-hour counts.
Vegetation soil-water storage alone did not change dangerous-heat-hour counts.
In this setup, the air-temperature hazard was more responsive to reflective surface changes than to isolated hydrological parameters.

turns.user_request: Make a website based on the experiments.
turns.assistant_recommendation: Use a dependency-free static GitHub Pages dashboard rather than a large custom app.
assistant_actions: Generated docs/assets/dashboard/dashboard-data.js from CSV outputs.
Created docs/dashboard.html.
Created docs/assets/dashboard/dashboard.css.
Created docs/assets/dashboard/dashboard.js.
Linked the dashboard from docs/index.md.
Ran JavaScript syntax and static consistency checks.
dashboard_features: Site plot and site-level experiment deltas.

turns.user_request: Make the website plain-English for decision-makers; fix the baseline bars; improve the site-level experiment detail section; add site descriptions; add a plain-English heat-risk explanation.
assistant_actions: Rewrote dashboard copy in plainer English.
Fixed the baseline bars by setting the inner bar elements to display as block elements.
Added a prominent highlighted site selector.
Added plain-English site descriptions for all ten neighbourhoods.
Added a How Risk Is Scored section explaining heat, people exposed and sensitivity.
Renamed technical experiment labels to decision-maker labels.
Ran syntax and static consistency checks.

turns.user_request: The Main Pages home did not seem to have changed.
assistant_actions: Checked local docs/index.md.
Verified origin/main.
Fetched the live GitHub Pages home page with curl.
Confirmed the live home page contained the dashboard link and was serving the dashboard commit.
turns.result: The live page had updated; the likely issue was browser caching. The assistant advised hard refresh or direct dashboard access.

turns.user_request: Export our transcript and commit it to the repo.
assistant_actions: Created a structured Markdown transcript for the analysis, experiment and dashboard session.
Committed it locally.

commit.message: Add analysis and dashboard session transcript
commit.push_status: not pushed at time of creation because push approval was rejected

turns.user_request: Upload a transcript in JSON format.
assistant_actions: Created this structured JSON transcript.
Prepared it for commit and upload.

known_limitations: This is a structured transcript-style export rather than a verbatim UI log.
The analysis uses a SuPy 2025.7.6 compatibility path rather than the dataset-requested SuPy 2026.6.5 runtime.
The nested data/uda-city-hackathon checkout is intentionally untracked.
